1.arabic: بسم الله الرحمن الرحيم تبارك الذي نزل الفرقان على عبده ليكون للعالمين نذيرا
1.english: Blessed is He who sent down the Criterion upon His Servant that he may be to the worlds a warner -
1.transliteration: Tabaarakal lazee nazzalal Furqaana 'alaa 'abdihee li yakoona lil'aalameena nazeera

1.arabic: الذي له ملك السماوات والأرض ولم يتخذ ولدا ولم يكن له شريك في الملك وخلق كل شيء فقدره تقديرا
1.english: He to whom belongs the dominion of the heavens and the earth and who has not taken a son and has not had a partner in dominion and has created each thing and determined it with [precise] determination.
1.transliteration: Allazee lahoo mulkus samaawaati wal ardi wa lam yattakhiz waladanw wa lam yakul lahoo shareekun filmulki wa khalaqa kulla shai'in faqaddarahoo taqdeeraa

1.arabic: واتخذوا من دونه آلهة لا يخلقون شيئا وهم يخلقون ولا يملكون لأنفسهم ضرا ولا نفعا ولا يملكون موتا ولا حياة ولا نشورا
1.english: But they have taken besides Him gods which create nothing, while they are created, and possess not for themselves any harm or benefit and possess not [power to cause] death or life or resurrection.
1.transliteration: Wattakhazoo min dooniheee aahihatal laa yakhluqoona shai'anw wa hum yukhlaqoona wa laa yamlikoona li anfusihim darranw wa laa naf'anw wa laa yamlikoona mawtanw wa laa hayaatanw wa laa nushooraa

1.arabic: وقال الذين كفروا إن هذا إلا إفك افتراه وأعانه عليه قوم آخرون ۖ فقد جاءوا ظلما وزورا
1.english: And those who disbelieve say, "This [Qur'an] is not except a falsehood he invented, and another people assisted him in it." But they have committed an injustice and a lie.
1.transliteration: Wa qaalal lazeena kafarooo in haazaaa illaaa ifkunif taraahu wa a'aanahoo 'alaihi qawmun aakharoona faqad jaaa'oo zulmanw wa zooraa

1.arabic: وقالوا أساطير الأولين اكتتبها فهي تملى عليه بكرة وأصيلا
1.english: And they say, "Legends of the former peoples which he has written down, and they are dictated to him morning and afternoon."
1.transliteration: Wa qaalooo asaateerul awwaleenak tatabahaa fahiya tumlaa 'alaihi bukratanw wa aseelaa

1.arabic: قل أنزله الذي يعلم السر في السماوات والأرض ۚ إنه كان غفورا رحيما
1.english: Say, [O Muhammad], "It has been revealed by He who knows [every] secret within the heavens and the earth. Indeed, He is ever Forgiving and Merciful."
1.transliteration: Qul anzalhul lazee ya'lamus sirra fis samaawaati wal-ard; innahoo kaana Ghafoorar Raheemaa

1.arabic: وقالوا مال هذا الرسول يأكل الطعام ويمشي في الأسواق ۙ لولا أنزل إليه ملك فيكون معه نذيرا
1.english: And they say, "What is this messenger that eats food and walks in the markets? Why was there not sent down to him an angel so he would be with him a warner?
1.transliteration: Wa qaaloo maa li haazar Rasooli yaakulut ta'aama wa yamshee fil aswaaq; law laaa unzila ilaihi malakun fa yakoona ma'ahoo nazeeraa

1.arabic: أو يلقى إليه كنز أو تكون له جنة يأكل منها ۚ وقال الظالمون إن تتبعون إلا رجلا مسحورا
1.english: Or [why is not] a treasure presented to him [from heaven], or does he [not] have a garden from which he eats?" And the wrongdoers say, "You follow not but a man affected by magic."
1.transliteration: Aw yulqaaa ilaihi kanzun aw takoonu lahoo jannatuny yaakulu minhaa; wa qaalaz zaalimoona in tattabi'oona illaa rajulam mas hooraa

1.arabic: انظر كيف ضربوا لك الأمثال فضلوا فلا يستطيعون سبيلا
1.english: Look how they strike for you comparisons; but they have strayed, so they cannot [find] a way.
1.transliteration: Unzur kaifa daraboo lakal amsaala fadalloo falaa yastatee'oona sabeelaa

1.arabic: تبارك الذي إن شاء جعل لك خيرا من ذلك جنات تجري من تحتها الأنهار ويجعل لك قصورا
1.english: Blessed is He who, if He willed, could have made for you [something] better than that - gardens beneath which rivers flow - and could make for you palaces.
1.transliteration: Tabaarakal lazeee in shaaa'a ja'ala laka khairam min zaalika jannaatin tajree min tahtihal anhaaru wa yaj'al laka qusooraa

1.arabic: بل كذبوا بالساعة ۖ وأعتدنا لمن كذب بالساعة سعيرا
1.english: But they have denied the Hour, and We have prepared for those who deny the Hour a Blaze.
1.transliteration: Bal kazzaboo bis Saa'ati wa a'tadnaa liman kazzaba bis Saa'ati sa'eeraa

1.arabic: إذا رأتهم من مكان بعيد سمعوا لها تغيظا وزفيرا
1.english: When the Hellfire sees them from a distant place, they will hear its fury and roaring.
1.transliteration: Izaa ra'at hum mim ma kaanim ba'eedin sami'oo lahaa taghaiyuzanw wa zafeeraa

1.arabic: وإذا ألقوا منها مكانا ضيقا مقرنين دعوا هنالك ثبورا
1.english: And when they are thrown into a narrow place therein bound in chains, they will cry out thereupon for destruction.
1.transliteration: Wa izaaa ulqoo minhaa makaanan daiyiqam muqar raneena da'aw hunaalika subooraa

1.arabic: لا تدعوا اليوم ثبورا واحدا وادعوا ثبورا كثيرا
1.english: [They will be told], "Do not cry this Day for one destruction but cry for much destruction."
1.transliteration: Laa tad'ul yawma subooranw waahidanw wad'oo subooran kaseeraa

1.arabic: قل أذلك خير أم جنة الخلد التي وعد المتقون ۚ كانت لهم جزاء ومصيرا
1.english: Say, "Is that better or the Garden of Eternity which is promised to the righteous? It will be for them a reward and destination.
1.transliteration: Qul azaalika khairun am Jannatul khuldil latee wu'idal muttaqoon; kaanat lahum jazaaa'anw wa maseeraa

1.arabic: لهم فيها ما يشاءون خالدين ۚ كان على ربك وعدا مسئولا
1.english: For them therein is whatever they wish, [while] abiding eternally. It is ever upon your Lord a promise [worthy to be] requested.
1.transliteration: Lahum feehaa maa yashaaa'oona khaalideen; kaana 'alaa Rabbika wa'dam mas'oolaa

1.arabic: ويوم يحشرهم وما يعبدون من دون الله فيقول أأنتم أضللتم عبادي هؤلاء أم هم ضلوا السبيل
1.english: And [mention] the Day He will gather them and that which they worship besides Allah and will say, "Did you mislead these, My servants, or did they [themselves] stray from the way?"
1.transliteration: Wa Yawma yahshuruhum wa maa ya'budoona min doonil lahi fa yaqoolu 'a-antum adlaltum 'ibaadee haaa'ulaaa'i am hum dallus sabeel

1.arabic: قالوا سبحانك ما كان ينبغي لنا أن نتخذ من دونك من أولياء ولكن متعتهم وآباءهم حتى نسوا الذكر وكانوا قوما بورا
1.english: They will say, "Exalted are You! It was not for us to take besides You any allies. But You provided comforts for them and their fathers until they forgot the message and became a people ruined."
1.transliteration: Qaaloo Subhaanaka maa kaana yambaghee lanaaa an nattakhiza min doonika min awliyaaa'a wa laakim matta'tahum wa aabaaa'ahum hattaa nasuz zikra wa kaanoo qawmam booraa

1.arabic: فقد كذبوكم بما تقولون فما تستطيعون صرفا ولا نصرا ۚ ومن يظلم منكم نذقه عذابا كبيرا
1.english: So they will deny you, [disbelievers], in what you say, and you cannot avert [punishment] or [find] help. And whoever commits injustice among you - We will make him taste a great punishment.
1.transliteration: Faqad kazzabookum bimaa taqooloona famaa tastatee'oona sarfanw wa laa nasraa; wa mai yazlim minkum nuziqhu 'azaaban kabeeraa

1.arabic: وما أرسلنا قبلك من المرسلين إلا إنهم ليأكلون الطعام ويمشون في الأسواق ۗ وجعلنا بعضكم لبعض فتنة أتصبرون ۗ وكان ربك بصيرا
1.english: And We did not send before you, [O Muhammad], any of the messengers except that they ate food and walked in the markets. And We have made some of you [people] as trial for others - will you have patience? And ever is your Lord, Seeing.
1.transliteration: Wa maaa arsalnaa qablaka minal mursaleena illaaa innahum la yaakuloonat ta'aama wa yamshoona fil aswaaq; wa ja'alnaa ba'dakum liba'din fitnatan atasbiroon; wa kaana Rabbuka Baseera

1.arabic: ۞ وقال الذين لا يرجون لقاءنا لولا أنزل علينا الملائكة أو نرى ربنا ۗ لقد استكبروا في أنفسهم وعتوا عتوا كبيرا
1.english: And those who do not expect the meeting with Us say, "Why were not angels sent down to us, or [why] do we [not] see our Lord?" They have certainly become arrogant within themselves and [become] insolent with great insolence.
1.transliteration: Wa qaalal lazeena laa yarjoona liqaaa'anaa law laaa unzila 'alainal malaaa'ikatu awnaraa Rabbanaa; laqadistakbaroo feee anfusihim wa 'ataw 'utuwwan kabeeraa

1.arabic: يوم يرون الملائكة لا بشرى يومئذ للمجرمين ويقولون حجرا محجورا
1.english: The day they see the angels - no good tidings will there be that day for the criminals, and [the angels] will say, "Prevented and inaccessible."
1.transliteration: Yawma yarawnal malaaa 'ikata laa bushraa Yawma'izil lilmujrimeena wa yaqooloona hijram mahjooraa

1.arabic: وقدمنا إلى ما عملوا من عمل فجعلناه هباء منثورا
1.english: And We will regard what they have done of deeds and make them as dust dispersed.
1.transliteration: Wa qadimnaaa ilaa maa 'amiloo min 'amalin faja'alnaahu habaaa'am mansooraa

1.arabic: أصحاب الجنة يومئذ خير مستقرا وأحسن مقيلا
1.english: The companions of Paradise, that Day, are [in] a better settlement and better resting place.
1.transliteration: As haabul jannati yawma'izin khairum mustaqar ranw wa ahsanu maqeela

1.arabic: ويوم تشقق السماء بالغمام ونزل الملائكة تنزيلا
1.english: And [mention] the Day when the heaven will split open with [emerging] clouds, and the angels will be sent down in successive descent.
1.transliteration: Wa Yawma tashaqqaqus samaaa'u bilghamaami wa nuzzilal malaaa'ikatu tanzeela

1.arabic: الملك يومئذ الحق للرحمن ۚ وكان يوما على الكافرين عسيرا
1.english: True sovereignty, that Day, is for the Most Merciful. And it will be upon the disbelievers a difficult Day.
1.transliteration: Almulku Yawma'izinil haqqu lir Rahmaan; wa kaana Yawman'alal kaafireena 'aseeraa

1.arabic: ويوم يعض الظالم على يديه يقول يا ليتني اتخذت مع الرسول سبيلا
1.english: And the Day the wrongdoer will bite on his hands [in regret] he will say, "Oh, I wish I had taken with the Messenger a way.
1.transliteration: Wa Yawma ya'adduz zaalimu 'alaa yadaihi yaqoolu yaa laitanit takhaztu ma'ar Rasooli sabeelaa

1.arabic: يا ويلتى ليتني لم أتخذ فلانا خليلا
1.english: Oh, woe to me! I wish I had not taken that one as a friend.
1.transliteration: Yaa wailataa laitanee lam attakhiz fulaanan khaleelaa

1.arabic: لقد أضلني عن الذكر بعد إذ جاءني ۗ وكان الشيطان للإنسان خذولا
1.english: He led me away from the remembrance after it had come to me. And ever is Satan, to man, a deserter."
1.transliteration: Laqad adallanee 'aniz zikri ba'da iz jaaa'anee; wa kaanash Shaitaanu lil insaani khazoolaa

1.arabic: وقال الرسول يا رب إن قومي اتخذوا هذا القرآن مهجورا
1.english: And the Messenger has said, "O my Lord, indeed my people have taken this Qur'an as [a thing] abandoned."
1.transliteration: Wa qaalar Rasoolu yaa Rabbi inna qawmit takhazoo haazal Qur-aana mahjooraa

1.arabic: وكذلك جعلنا لكل نبي عدوا من المجرمين ۗ وكفى بربك هاديا ونصيرا
1.english: And thus have We made for every prophet an enemy from among the criminals. But sufficient is your Lord as a guide and a helper.
1.transliteration: Wa kazaalika ja'alnaa likulli Nabiyyin 'aduwwam minal mujrimeen; wa kafaa bi Rabbika haadiyanw wa naseeraa

1.arabic: وقال الذين كفروا لولا نزل عليه القرآن جملة واحدة ۚ كذلك لنثبت به فؤادك ۖ ورتلناه ترتيلا
1.english: And those who disbelieve say, "Why was the Qur'an not revealed to him all at once?" Thus [it is] that We may strengthen thereby your heart. And We have spaced it distinctly.
1.transliteration: Wa qaalal lazeena kafaroo law laa nuzzila 'alaihil Quraanu jumlatanw waahidah; kazaalika linusabbita bihee fu'aadaka wa rattalnaahu tarteelaa

1.arabic: ولا يأتونك بمثل إلا جئناك بالحق وأحسن تفسيرا
1.english: And they do not come to you with an argument except that We bring you the truth and the best explanation.
1.transliteration: Wa laa yaatoonaka bimasainn illaa ji'naaka bilhaqqi wa ahsana tafseeraa

1.arabic: الذين يحشرون على وجوههم إلى جهنم أولئك شر مكانا وأضل سبيلا
1.english: The ones who are gathered on their faces to Hell - those are the worst in position and farthest astray in [their] way.
1.transliteration: Allazeena yuhsharoona 'alaa wujoohim ilaa jahannama ulaaa'ika sharrum makaananw wa adallu sabeelaa )section 3)

1.arabic: ولقد آتينا موسى الكتاب وجعلنا معه أخاه هارون وزيرا
1.english: And We had certainly given Moses the Scripture and appointed with him his brother Aaron as an assistant.
1.transliteration: Wa laqad aatainaa Moosal Kitaaba wa ja'alnaa ma'ahooo akhaahu Haaroona wazeeraa

1.arabic: فقلنا اذهبا إلى القوم الذين كذبوا بآياتنا فدمرناهم تدميرا
1.english: And We said, "Go both of you to the people who have denied Our signs." Then We destroyed them with [complete] destruction.
1.transliteration: Faqulnaz habaaa ilal qawmil lazeena kazzaboo bi Aayaatinaa fadammarnaahum tadmeeraa

1.arabic: وقوم نوح لما كذبوا الرسل أغرقناهم وجعلناهم للناس آية ۖ وأعتدنا للظالمين عذابا أليما
1.english: And the people of Noah - when they denied the messengers, We drowned them, and We made them for mankind a sign. And We have prepared for the wrongdoers a painful punishment.
1.transliteration: Wa qawma Noohil lammaa kazzabur Rusula aghraqnaahum wa ja'alnaahum linnaasi Aayatanw wa a'tadnaa lizzaalimeena 'azaaban aleemaa

1.arabic: وعادا وثمود وأصحاب الرس وقرونا بين ذلك كثيرا
1.english: And [We destroyed] 'Aad and Thamud and the companions of the well and many generations between them.
1.transliteration: Wa 'Aadanw Samooda wa As haabar Rassi wa quroonam baina zaalika kaseeraa

1.arabic: وكلا ضربنا له الأمثال ۖ وكلا تبرنا تتبيرا
1.english: And for each We presented examples [as warnings], and each We destroyed with [total] destruction.
1.transliteration: Wa kullandarabnaa lahul amsaala wa kullan tabbarnaa tatbeera

1.arabic: ولقد أتوا على القرية التي أمطرت مطر السوء ۚ أفلم يكونوا يرونها ۚ بل كانوا لا يرجون نشورا
1.english: And they have already come upon the town which was showered with a rain of evil. So have they not seen it? But they are not expecting resurrection.
1.transliteration: Wa laqad ataw 'alal qaryatil lateee umtirat mataras saw'; afalam yakoonoo yarawnahaa; bal kaanoo laa yarjoona nushooraa

1.arabic: وإذا رأوك إن يتخذونك إلا هزوا أهذا الذي بعث الله رسولا
1.english: And when they see you, [O Muhammad], they take you not except in ridicule, [saying], "Is this the one whom Allah has sent as a messenger?
1.transliteration: Wa izaa ra awka iny yattakhizoonaka illaa huzuwan ahaazal lazee ba'asal laahu Rasoolaa

1.arabic: إن كاد ليضلنا عن آلهتنا لولا أن صبرنا عليها ۚ وسوف يعلمون حين يرون العذاب من أضل سبيلا
1.english: He almost would have misled us from our gods had we not been steadfast in [worship of] them." But they are going to know, when they see the punishment, who is farthest astray in [his] way.
1.transliteration: In kaada la yudillunaa 'an aalihatinaa law laaa an sabarnaa 'alaihaa; wa sawfa ya'lamoona heena yarawnal 'azaaba man adallu sabeela

1.arabic: أرأيت من اتخذ إلهه هواه أفأنت تكون عليه وكيلا
1.english: Have you seen the one who takes as his god his own desire? Then would you be responsible for him?
1.transliteration: Ara'aita manit takhaza ilaahahoo hawaahu afa anta takoonu 'alaihi wakeelaa

1.arabic: أم تحسب أن أكثرهم يسمعون أو يعقلون ۚ إن هم إلا كالأنعام ۖ بل هم أضل سبيلا
1.english: Or do you think that most of them hear or reason? They are not except like livestock. Rather, they are [even] more astray in [their] way.
1.transliteration: Am tahsabu annna aksarahum yasma'oona aw ya''qiloon; in hum illaa kal an'aami bal hum adallu sabeelan

1.arabic: ألم تر إلى ربك كيف مد الظل ولو شاء لجعله ساكنا ثم جعلنا الشمس عليه دليلا
1.english: Have you not considered your Lord - how He extends the shadow, and if He willed, He could have made it stationary? Then We made the sun for it an indication.
1.transliteration: Alam tara ilaa Rabbika kaifa maddaz zilla wa law shaaa'a laja'alahoo saakinan summa ja'alnash shamsa 'alaihe daleelaa

1.arabic: ثم قبضناه إلينا قبضا يسيرا
1.english: Then We hold it in hand for a brief grasp.
1.transliteration: Summa qabadnaahu ilainaa qabdany yaseeraa

1.arabic: وهو الذي جعل لكم الليل لباسا والنوم سباتا وجعل النهار نشورا
1.english: And it is He who has made the night for you as clothing and sleep [a means for] rest and has made the day a resurrection.
1.transliteration: Wa Huwal lazee ja'ala lakumul laila libaasanw wannawma subaatanw wa ja'alan nahaara nushooraa

1.arabic: وهو الذي أرسل الرياح بشرا بين يدي رحمته ۚ وأنزلنا من السماء ماء طهورا
1.english: And it is He who sends the winds as good tidings before His mercy, and We send down from the sky pure water
1.transliteration: Wa Huwal lazeee arsalar riyaaha bushram baina yadai rahmatih; wa anzalnaa minas samaaa'i maaa'an tahooraa

1.arabic: لنحيي به بلدة ميتا ونسقيه مما خلقنا أنعاما وأناسي كثيرا
1.english: That We may bring to life thereby a dead land and give it as drink to those We created of numerous livestock and men.
1.transliteration: Linuhyiya bihee balda tam maitanw wa nusqiyahoo mimmaa khalaqnaaa an'aa manw wa anaasiyya kaseeraa

1.arabic: ولقد صرفناه بينهم ليذكروا فأبى أكثر الناس إلا كفورا
1.english: And We have certainly distributed it among them that they might be reminded, but most of the people refuse except disbelief.
1.transliteration: Wa laqad sarrafnaahu bainahum li yazzakkaroo fa abaaa aksarun naasi illaa kufooraa

1.arabic: ولو شئنا لبعثنا في كل قرية نذيرا
1.english: And if We had willed, We could have sent into every city a warner.
1.transliteration: Wa law shi'naa laba'asnaa fee kulli qar yatin nazeeraa

1.arabic: فلا تطع الكافرين وجاهدهم به جهادا كبيرا
1.english: So do not obey the disbelievers, and strive against them with the Qur'an a great striving.
1.transliteration: Falaa tuti'il kaafireena wa jaahidhum bihee jihaadan kabeeraa

1.arabic: ۞ وهو الذي مرج البحرين هذا عذب فرات وهذا ملح أجاج وجعل بينهما برزخا وحجرا محجورا
1.english: And it is He who has released [simultaneously] the two seas, one fresh and sweet and one salty and bitter, and He placed between them a barrier and prohibiting partition.
1.transliteration: Wa Huwal lazee marajal bahraini haazaa 'azbun furaatunw wa haazaa milhun ujaaj; wa ja'ala bainahumaa barzakhanw wa hijram mahjooraa

1.arabic: وهو الذي خلق من الماء بشرا فجعله نسبا وصهرا ۗ وكان ربك قديرا
1.english: And it is He who has created from water a human being and made him [a relative by] lineage and marriage. And ever is your Lord competent [concerning creation].
1.transliteration: Wa Huwal lazee khalaqa minal maaa'i basharran fa ja'alahoo nasaban wa sihraa; wa kaana Rabbuka Qadeeraa

1.arabic: ويعبدون من دون الله ما لا ينفعهم ولا يضرهم ۗ وكان الكافر على ربه ظهيرا
1.english: But they worship rather than Allah that which does not benefit them or harm them, and the disbeliever is ever, against his Lord, an assistant [to Satan].
1.transliteration: Wa ya'budoona min doonil laahi maa laa yanfa'uhum wa laa yadurruhum; wa kaanal kaafiru 'alaa Rabbihee zaheeraa

1.arabic: وما أرسلناك إلا مبشرا ونذيرا
1.english: And We have not sent you, [O Muhammad], except as a bringer of good tidings and a warner.
1.transliteration: Wa maa arsalnaaka illaa mubashshiranw wa nazeeraa

1.arabic: قل ما أسألكم عليه من أجر إلا من شاء أن يتخذ إلى ربه سبيلا
1.english: Say, "I do not ask of you for it any payment - only that whoever wills might take to his Lord a way."
1.transliteration: Qul maaa as'alukum 'alaihi min ajrin illaa man shaaa'a ai yattakhiza ilaa Rabbihee sabeelaa

1.arabic: وتوكل على الحي الذي لا يموت وسبح بحمده ۚ وكفى به بذنوب عباده خبيرا
1.english: And rely upon the Ever-Living who does not die, and exalt [Allah] with His praise. And sufficient is He to be, with the sins of His servants, Acquainted -
1.transliteration: Wa tawakkal 'alal Haiyil lazee laa yamootu wa sabbih bihamdih; wa kafaa bihee bizunoobi 'ibaadihee khabeeraa

1.arabic: الذي خلق السماوات والأرض وما بينهما في ستة أيام ثم استوى على العرش ۚ الرحمن فاسأل به خبيرا
1.english: He who created the heavens and the earth and what is between them in six days and then established Himself above the Throne - the Most Merciful, so ask about Him one well informed.
1.transliteration: Allazee khalaqas samaawaati wal arda wa maa bainahumaa fee sittati aiyaamin summmmastawaa 'alal 'Arsh; ar Rahmaanu fas'al bihee khabeeraa

1.arabic: وإذا قيل لهم اسجدوا للرحمن قالوا وما الرحمن أنسجد لما تأمرنا وزادهم نفورا ۩
1.english: And when it is said to them, "Prostrate to the Most Merciful," they say, "And what is the Most Merciful? Should we prostrate to that which you order us?" And it increases them in aversion.
1.transliteration: Wa izaa qeela lahumus judoo lir Rahmaani qaaloo wa mar Rahmaanu anasjudu limaa taamurunaa wa zaadahum nufooraa

1.arabic: تبارك الذي جعل في السماء بروجا وجعل فيها سراجا وقمرا منيرا
1.english: Blessed is He who has placed in the sky great stars and placed therein a [burning] lamp and luminous moon.
1.transliteration: Tabaarakal lazee ja'ala fis samaaa'i buroojanw wa ja'ala feehaa siraajanw wa qamaram muneeraa

1.arabic: وهو الذي جعل الليل والنهار خلفة لمن أراد أن يذكر أو أراد شكورا
1.english: And it is He who has made the night and the day in succession for whoever desires to remember or desires gratitude.
1.transliteration: Wa huwal lazee ja'alal laila wannahaara khilfatal liman araada ai yazzakkara aw araadaa shookooraa

1.arabic: وعباد الرحمن الذين يمشون على الأرض هونا وإذا خاطبهم الجاهلون قالوا سلاما
1.english: And the servants of the Most Merciful are those who walk upon the earth easily, and when the ignorant address them [harshly], they say [words of] peace,
1.transliteration: Wa 'ibaadur Rahmaanil lazeena yamshoona 'alal ardi hawnanw wa izaa khaata bahumul jaahiloona qaaloo salaamaa

1.arabic: والذين يبيتون لربهم سجدا وقياما
1.english: And those who spend [part of] the night to their Lord prostrating and standing [in prayer]
1.transliteration: Wallazeena yabeetoona li Rabbihim sujjadanw wa qiyaamaa

1.arabic: والذين يقولون ربنا اصرف عنا عذاب جهنم ۖ إن عذابها كان غراما
1.english: And those who say, "Our Lord, avert from us the punishment of Hell. Indeed, its punishment is ever adhering;
1.transliteration: Wallazeena yaqooloona Rabbanas rif 'annnaa 'azaaba Jahannama inn 'azaabahaa kaana gharaamaa

1.arabic: إنها ساءت مستقرا ومقاما
1.english: Indeed, it is evil as a settlement and residence."
1.transliteration: Innahaa saaa'at mustaqarranw wa muqaamaa

1.arabic: والذين إذا أنفقوا لم يسرفوا ولم يقتروا وكان بين ذلك قواما
1.english: And [they are] those who, when they spend, do so not excessively or sparingly but are ever, between that, [justly] moderate
1.transliteration: Wallazeena izaaa anfaqoo lam yusrifoo wa lam yaqturoo wa kaana baina zaalika qawaamaa

1.arabic: والذين لا يدعون مع الله إلها آخر ولا يقتلون النفس التي حرم الله إلا بالحق ولا يزنون ۚ ومن يفعل ذلك يلق أثاما
1.english: And those who do not invoke with Allah another deity or kill the soul which Allah has forbidden [to be killed], except by right, and do not commit unlawful sexual intercourse. And whoever should do that will meet a penalty.
1.transliteration: Wallazeena laa yad'oona ma'al laahi ilaahan aakhara wa laa yaqtuloonan nafsal latee harramal laahu illaa bilhaqqi wa laa yaznoon; wa mai yaf'al zaalika yalqa asaamaa

1.arabic: يضاعف له العذاب يوم القيامة ويخلد فيه مهانا
1.english: Multiplied for him is the punishment on the Day of Resurrection, and he will abide therein humiliated -
1.transliteration: Yudaa'af lahul 'azaabu Yawmal Qiyaamati wa yakhlud feehee muhaanaa

1.arabic: إلا من تاب وآمن وعمل عملا صالحا فأولئك يبدل الله سيئاتهم حسنات ۗ وكان الله غفورا رحيما
1.english: Except for those who repent, believe and do righteous work. For them Allah will replace their evil deeds with good. And ever is Allah Forgiving and Merciful.
1.transliteration: Illaa man taaba wa 'aamana wa 'amila 'amalan saalihan fa ulaaa'ika yubad dilul laahu saiyi aatihim hasanaat; wa kaanal laahu Ghafoorar Raheemaa

1.arabic: ومن تاب وعمل صالحا فإنه يتوب إلى الله متابا
1.english: And he who repents and does righteousness does indeed turn to Allah with [accepted] repentance.
1.transliteration: Wa man taaba wa 'amila saalihan fa innnahoo yatoobu ilal laahi mataabaa

1.arabic: والذين لا يشهدون الزور وإذا مروا باللغو مروا كراما
1.english: And [they are] those who do not testify to falsehood, and when they pass near ill speech, they pass by with dignity.
1.transliteration: Wallazeena laa yash hadoonaz zoora wa izaa marroo billaghwi marroo kiraamaa

1.arabic: والذين إذا ذكروا بآيات ربهم لم يخروا عليها صما وعميانا
1.english: And those who, when reminded of the verses of their Lord, do not fall upon them deaf and blind.
1.transliteration: Wallazeena izaa zukkiroo bi Aayaati Rabbihim lam yakhirroo 'alaihaa summanw wa'umyaanaa

1.arabic: والذين يقولون ربنا هب لنا من أزواجنا وذرياتنا قرة أعين واجعلنا للمتقين إماما
1.english: And those who say, "Our Lord, grant us from among our wives and offspring comfort to our eyes and make us an example for the righteous."
1.transliteration: Wallazeena yaqooloona Rabbanaa hab lanaa min azwaajinaa wa zurriyaatinaa qurrata a'yuninw waj 'alnaa lilmuttaqeena Imaamaa

1.arabic: أولئك يجزون الغرفة بما صبروا ويلقون فيها تحية وسلاما
1.english: Those will be awarded the Chamber for what they patiently endured, and they will be received therein with greetings and [words of] peace.
1.transliteration: Ulaaa'ika yujzawnal ghurfata bimaa sabaroo wa yulaqqawna feehaa tahiyyatanw wa salaamaa

1.arabic: خالدين فيها ۚ حسنت مستقرا ومقاما
1.english: Abiding eternally therein. Good is the settlement and residence.
1.transliteration: Khaalideena feehaa; hasunat mustaqarranw wa muqaamaa

1.arabic: قل ما يعبأ بكم ربي لولا دعاؤكم ۖ فقد كذبتم فسوف يكون لزاما
1.english: Say, "What would my Lord care for you if not for your supplication?" For you [disbelievers] have denied, so your denial is going to be adherent.
1.transliteration: Qul maa ya'ba'u bikum Rabbee law laa du'aaa'ukum faqad kazzabtum fasawfa yakoonu lizaamaa
